proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher age_years is associated with longer pfs_months (positive linear association across the full age range).
Mean pfs_months differs between female (sex_female=1) and male (sex_female=0) patients.

analyses.code: from scipy import stats; stats.linregress(df['age_years'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Univariate linear regression: PFS increases ~0.176 months per additional year of age (mean PFS ~0.3 mo at age 30-35 rising monotonically to ~8.6 mo at age 85-90).
Mean PFS 4.308 mo in females vs 4.314 mo in males (Welch t-test).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Worse performance status (higher ecog_ps) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
stage_iv=1 patients have shorter pfs_months than stage_iv=0 patients.
right_sided_primary=1 patients have shorter pfs_months than right_sided_primary=0 (left-sided) patients.

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS 5.27 / 4.05 / 2.91 months at ECOG 0/1/2 respectively. Linear slope -1.19 months per ECOG unit.
Mean PFS 3.70 mo in stage IV vs 5.06 mo in stage III (difference -1.35 mo).
Mean PFS 4.11 mo in right-sided vs 4.42 mo in left-sided primaries (difference -0.31 mo).

proposed_hypotheses.text: kras_mutation=1 patients have shorter pfs_months than kras_mutation=0.
braf_v600e=1 patients have shorter pfs_months than braf_v600e=0.
nras_mutation=1 patients differ in pfs_months from nras_mutation=0.
msi_high=1 patients differ in pfs_months from msi_high=0.
her2_amplified=1 patients differ in pfs_months from her2_amplified=0.
ntrk_fusion=1 patients differ in pfs_months from ntrk_fusion=0.

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS 4.12 mo in KRAS-mutant vs 4.45 mo in KRAS WT (difference -0.33 mo).
Mean PFS 4.09 mo in BRAF V600E vs 4.32 mo in WT (difference -0.23 mo).
Mean PFS 4.53 mo in NRAS+ vs 4.31 mo in NRAS- (difference +0.22 mo); statistically significant but small.
Mean PFS 4.29 mo in MSI-high vs 4.31 mo in MSS (difference -0.02 mo).
Mean PFS 4.26 mo in HER2-amplified vs 4.31 mo in non-amplified (difference -0.06 mo).
Mean PFS 4.35 mo in NTRK-fusion vs 4.31 mo in non-fusion (difference +0.04 mo, n=251).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher cea_ng_ml is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months.
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Greater weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher crp_mg_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher nlr is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Other labs (hemoglobin, alkaline phosphatase, AST, ALT, bilirubin, creatinine, BUN, sodium, potassium, calcium) are associated with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Pearson r = -0.038 between CEA and PFS (small but highly significant negative correlation).
Pearson r = +0.102 between albumin and PFS; strong positive association.
Pearson r = -0.011 between LDH and PFS (small but significant negative).
Pearson r = -0.123 between 6-month weight loss and PFS; strong negative association.
Pearson r = -0.008 (borderline non-significant univariately).
Pearson r = +0.005 (no association).

analyses.code: Pearson r for hemoglobin, alkaline_phosphatase, ast, alt, total_bilirubin, creatinine, bun, sodium, potassium, calcium
analyses.result_summary: All other labs show r in [-0.013, +0.009]; only alkaline_phosphatase (-0.013, p=0.004) and total_bilirubin (-0.009, p=0.044) reach univariate significance, none clinically meaningful.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_cetuximab=1 have different pfs_months than those with treatment_cetuximab=0 (overall main effect).
Patients receiving treatment_bevacizumab=1 have different pfs_months than those with treatment_bevacizumab=0.
Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab=1 have different pfs_months than those with treatment_pembrolizumab=0.
Patients receiving treatment_encorafenib=1 have different pfs_months than those with treatment_encorafenib=0.
Patients receiving treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib=1 have different pfs_months than those with treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib=0.
Patients receiving treatment_regorafenib=1 have longer pfs_months than those with treatment_regorafenib=0.

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS 4.285 (cetuximab) vs 4.323 (no cetuximab); difference -0.038 mo, p=0.088.
Mean PFS 4.301 (bev) vs 4.320 (no bev); difference -0.019 mo, p=0.354.
Mean PFS 4.316 (pembro) vs 4.311 (no pembro); difference +0.005 mo, p=0.856.
Mean PFS 4.316 (enco) vs 4.311 (no enco); difference +0.005 mo, p=0.873.
Mean PFS 4.271 (trast/tuca) vs 4.315 (no); difference -0.044 mo, p=0.244.
Mean PFS 5.089 (rego) vs 4.117 (no rego); difference +0.972 mo. The only treatment with a clear cohort-wide main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable OLS model of pfs_months on all 33 candidate predictors (including all six treatments), the strongest independent predictors of pfs_months are age_years (positive), ecog_ps (negative), stage_iv (negative), albumin_g_dl (positive), weight_loss_pct_6mo (negative), kras_mutation (negative), braf_v600e (negative), right_sided_primary (negative), cea_ng_ml (negative), and treatment_regorafenib (positive); other treatments (cetuximab, bevacizumab, pembrolizumab, encorafenib, trastuzumab_tucatinib) have null adjusted main effects.

analyses.result_summary: Multivariable OLS R^2 = 0.859. Significant adjusted betas (p<0.001): age_years +0.176/yr, ecog_ps -1.171, stage_iv -1.362, right_sided_primary -0.287, kras_mutation -0.354, braf_v600e -0.357, cea_ng_ml -0.005, albumin_g_dl +0.469, ldh_u_l -0.0004, weight_loss_pct_6mo -0.076, treatment_regorafenib +0.943. Other treatments and biomarkers (sex, NRAS, MSI, HER2, NTRK) and the rest of the labs are non-significant when adjusted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cetuximab benefits pfs_months only in kras_mutation=0 patients (textbook anti-EGFR pattern).
Cetuximab differentially affects pfs_months by braf_v600e status.
Cetuximab differentially affects pfs_months by nras_mutation status.
Cetuximab benefits pfs_months only in left-sided (right_sided_primary=0) patients.

analyses.code: Stratified t-tests of treatment_cetuximab within kras_mutation strata
analyses.result_summary: KRAS WT: cetux 4.415 vs no-cetux 4.463 (diff -0.048, p=0.122). KRAS mut: cetux 4.102 vs no-cetux 4.130 (diff -0.028, p=0.376). No benefit in either stratum.
BRAF WT: cetux 4.296 vs 4.333 (diff -0.037, p=0.111). BRAF V600E: cetux 4.051 vs 4.113 (diff -0.063, p=0.507). No benefit.
NRAS WT: cetux 4.279 vs 4.316 (diff -0.037, p=0.098). NRAS mut: cetux 4.495 vs 4.539 (diff -0.044, p=0.757). No benefit.
Left-sided: cetux 4.385 vs 4.434 (diff -0.049, p=0.086). Right-sided: cetux 4.098 vs 4.121 (diff -0.024, p=0.490). Cetuximab has no detectable benefit in any subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Pembrolizumab improves pfs_months in msi_high=1 patients (textbook checkpoint inhibitor pattern).
Even within msi_high=1, pembrolizumab improves pfs_months in finer subgroups defined by stage_iv, right_sided_primary, kras_mutation, or braf_v600e.

analyses.result_summary: MSI-H: pembro 4.299 vs 4.292 (diff +0.007, p=0.957). MSS: pembro 4.317 vs 4.312 (diff +0.005, p=0.861). Pembrolizumab gives no detectable PFS benefit in MSI-H.

analyses.code: Stratified t-tests of pembro within MSI-H by stage/sidedness/KRAS/BRAF
analyses.result_summary: All eight 2x2 sub-strata of MSI-H by stage/sidedness/KRAS/BRAF show |delta| <= 0.20 mo and p>=0.28. Pembrolizumab does not benefit any pre-specified MSI-H subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Encorafenib improves pfs_months in braf_v600e=1 patients (textbook BRAF inhibitor pattern).
Encorafenib + cetuximab combined gives longer pfs_months than neither in braf_v600e=1 patients (per BEACON CRC).

analyses.result_summary: BRAF V600E: enco 3.979 vs 4.108 (diff -0.129, p=0.329). BRAF WT: enco 4.333 vs 4.321 (diff +0.013, p=0.718). No benefit; numerically slightly worse in V600E.

analyses.code: Mean PFS in BRAF V600E with both vs neither cetuximab+encorafenib
analyses.result_summary: BRAF V600E receiving both encorafenib AND cetuximab: 4.151 (n=58); receiving neither: 4.138 (n=1390). Difference negligible (+0.013 mo).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Trastuzumab/tucatinib improves pfs_months in her2_amplified=1 patients (textbook anti-HER2 pattern).

analyses.result_summary: HER2+: trast/tuca 4.273 vs 4.256 (diff +0.017, p=0.934). HER2-: trast/tuca 4.271 vs 4.317 (diff -0.046, p=0.232). No HER2-selective benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Bevacizumab improves pfs_months in some clinically meaningful subgroup defined by stage_iv x right_sided_primary.

analyses.code: Stratified t-tests of bev within stage_iv x right_sided_primary cells
analyses.result_summary: All four cells (stage III/IV x left/right): |bev effect| <= 0.05 mo, p>=0.31. Bevacizumab is null across stage and sidedness combinations.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The regorafenib pfs_months benefit is concentrated in kras_mutation=0 patients and is absent in kras_mutation=1.
The regorafenib pfs_months benefit is absent or reversed in braf_v600e=1 patients.
The regorafenib pfs_months benefit is concentrated in left-sided (right_sided_primary=0) patients and is absent in right-sided.
MSI, HER2, NTRK, and NRAS do NOT modify the regorafenib pfs_months effect.

analyses.code: Stratified t-tests of regorafenib within kras_mutation strata
analyses.result_summary: KRAS WT: rego 5.773 vs 4.117 (diff +1.656, p=6.1e-293). KRAS mut: rego 4.143 vs 4.116 (diff +0.027, p=0.456). Strong qualitative interaction.
BRAF WT: rego 5.144 vs 4.116 (diff +1.027, p=5.2e-226). BRAF V600E: rego 3.971 vs 4.126 (diff -0.156, p=0.134). Qualitative interaction; rego inactive (slightly numerically worse) in V600E.
Left-sided: rego 5.601 vs 4.121 (diff +1.480, p=3.7e-274). Right-sided: rego 4.135 vs 4.109 (diff +0.026, p=0.516). Qualitative interaction with sidedness.

analyses.code: Stratified t-tests within msi_high, her2_amplified, ntrk_fusion, nras_mutation strata
analyses.result_summary: Regorafenib effect is similar across MSI-H/MSS (+0.95/+0.97), HER2+/- (+0.87/+0.98), NTRK fusion+/- (+1.44 in tiny subset/+0.97), and NRAS+/- (+1.93/+0.94). None of these biomarkers modify the rego effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Regorafenib improves pfs_months ONLY in patients who simultaneously have kras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0 (the 'triple-favorable' subgroup); the effect is essentially zero in any patient who is kras_mutation=1 OR braf_v600e=1 OR right_sided_primary=1.
In a kras_mutation x right_sided_primary 2x2 table, regorafenib improves pfs_months only in the (kras=0, right=0) cell.

analyses.code: Triple-stratified t-test: regorafenib effect within (kras_mutation==0 & braf_v600e==0 & right_sided_primary==0) vs the complement.
analyses.result_summary: In the triple-favorable subgroup (n=17372, ~35% of cohort): rego 6.875 vs no-rego 4.117 (diff +2.758, p<<1e-300). In the complement subgroup (any unfavorable: kras=1 OR braf=1 OR right=1, n=32628): rego 4.147 vs 4.117 (diff +0.030, p=0.294). Pure qualitative interaction.

analyses.code: 2x2 stratification on kras_mutation and right_sided_primary, t-test of regorafenib within each cell.
analyses.result_summary: (KRAS=0, right=0): rego 6.639 vs 4.119 (diff +2.520, p~0). (KRAS=0, right=1): diff +0.071, p=0.163. (KRAS=1, right=0): diff +0.061, p=0.169. (KRAS=1, right=1): diff -0.040, p=0.521. Effect concentrated in (KRAS=0, right=0) only.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable OLS model that splits treatment_regorafenib into 'rego_in_subgroup' (rego x triple-favorable indicator) and 'rego_outside' (rego x complement), only rego_in_subgroup is associated with longer pfs_months; rego_outside is null.

After adjusting for the rego_in_subgroup interaction term, the apparent main effects of kras_mutation, braf_v600e, and right_sided_primary on pfs_months largely disappear, indicating those associations are mediated by their predicting non-benefit from regorafenib.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ all covariates + rego_in_subgroup + rego_outside (instead of treatment_regorafenib alone)
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted beta for rego_in_subgroup = +2.736 (p<<0.001); rego_outside = -0.003 (p=0.78). All other treatments (cetuximab, bev, pembro, enco, trast/tuca) remain null (|beta|<=0.022).

analyses.code: Compare adjusted betas of kras_mutation/braf_v600e/right_sided_primary in OLS with vs without the rego_in_subgroup split.
analyses.result_summary: Without the rego subgroup split: kras=-0.354, braf=-0.357, right=-0.287 (all p<<0.001). With the rego_in_subgroup split: kras=-0.00004, braf=+0.004, right=+0.002 (all p>0.7). Among non-rego patients alone the same biomarkers are also null (kras diff=-0.001 p=0.95; braf +0.010 p=0.85; right -0.012 p=0.59). Thus the cohort-wide negative associations of these three biomarkers with PFS are entirely explained by their identifying patients who do not benefit from regorafenib.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the triple-favorable rego subgroup, the regorafenib pfs_months benefit is attenuated as cea_ng_ml increases.
The regorafenib treatment effect varies with age_years.
Regorafenib treatment effect varies with albumin_g_dl, ldh_u_l, weight_loss_pct_6mo, crp_mg_l, nlr, hemoglobin_g_dl, or alkaline_phosphatase_u_l.

analyses.code: OLS within triple-favorable subgroup: pfs_months ~ covariates + treatment_regorafenib + treatment_regorafenib*centered_cea_ng_ml
analyses.result_summary: Within the triple-favorable subgroup, rego x CEA interaction beta = -0.064 per ng/mL (p<<0.001); rego main effect remains +2.73. Outside the subgroup the interaction is essentially zero (-0.00001, p=0.97). Higher CEA dampens the rego benefit but does not eliminate the qualitative interaction with the triple subgroup.
Rego x age interaction beta = +0.0011 per year (p=0.264). Regorafenib effect does not vary significantly with age.

analyses.code: OLS interaction screen across continuous predictors (centered) x each treatment
analyses.result_summary: Significant treatment-by-continuous interactions (full-cohort): rego x cea_ng_ml beta=-0.026 (p<<0.001); rego x albumin_g_dl beta=-0.078 (p=5e-5); rego x nlr beta=+0.017 (p=3e-5). No other treatment showed any significant continuous interaction. The rego x continuous effects are largely driven by the same triple-favorable subgroup confounding (CEA being elevated and albumin lower in worse-disease patients).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The regorafenib benefit within the triple-favorable subgroup is approximately constant in absolute magnitude across ECOG performance status levels (0, 1, 2).
Within the triple-favorable subgroup, NRAS mutation status does not change the regorafenib effect.

analyses.code: Stratified t-tests of regorafenib by ECOG within triple-favorable subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Within triple-favorable: ECOG 0 rego 7.757 vs 5.092 (diff +2.665), ECOG 1 rego 6.613 vs 3.847 (diff +2.765), ECOG 2 rego 5.445 vs 2.702 (diff +2.743). Absolute magnitude is essentially constant across ECOG (~2.7 mo).

analyses.code: Stratified t-test of regorafenib within triple-favorable x NRAS
analyses.result_summary: Triple-favorable, NRAS WT: rego 6.866 vs 4.117 (diff +2.749). Triple-favorable, NRAS mut: rego 7.018 vs 4.112 (diff +2.906). NRAS does not modify the rego effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT SUBGROUP HYPOTHESIS: treatment_regorafenib increases pfs_months by approximately +2.74 months (95% CI roughly [2.71, 2.76] from OLS) ONLY in the subgroup of patients who simultaneously satisfy kras_mutation==0 AND braf_v600e==0 AND right_sided_primary==0; in any patient with kras_mutation==1 OR braf_v600e==1 OR right_sided_primary==1, the regorafenib effect on pfs_months is zero (point estimate near 0, p=0.29). No other treatment in this cohort (cetuximab, bevacizumab, pembrolizumab, encorafenib, trastuzumab_tucatinib) shows a clinically or statistically meaningful pfs_months effect overall or in any pre-specified biomarker subgroup.

After adjusting for ECOG, stage, biomarkers, labs, and all treatments (including the rego_in_subgroup split), age_years remains independently positively associated with pfs_months at +0.176 months per year.

analyses.result_summary: FINAL: regorafenib effect = +2.758 mo in (KRAS WT & BRAF WT & left-sided), n=17372, p<<1e-300; +0.030 mo in any unfavorable predicate, n=32628, p=0.294. Cohort-wide observed effect (+0.97) equals the within-subgroup effect (+2.76) times subgroup prevalence (~0.35). All other treatments null overall and in subgroups.

analyses.code: Multivariable OLS with rego_in_subgroup split: coefficient on age_years
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted age_years beta = +0.1761 mo/yr (p<<0.001). The positive age effect is robust to adjustment for all measured prognostic factors and treatment effects, indicating it is a strong primary feature of this dataset (PFS rises monotonically from ~0.3 mo in patients aged 30-35 to ~8.6 mo in patients aged 85-90).
